content:
{
  "market_assessment": {
    "bias": "BULLISH",
    "reasoning": "Broad rally with strong sector breadth — defense (GRSE +9.2%, HAL +1.55%), pharma (BIOCON +5.66%, AUROPHARMA +7.61%), chemicals (NAVINFLUOR +3.72%, SRF +6.85%), FMCG (MARICO +4.84%), financials (ABCAPITAL +6.61%, CAMS +10.46%) all participating. Multiple positions are running in our favor with extended moves. Priority is to lock gains via trailing SLs on winners while respecting the 1% SL-to-CMP gap discipline.",
    "key_levels": {
      "nifty_support": 23800,
      "nifty_resistance": 24400
    }
  },

      "confidence": 0.65,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "reasoning": "CMP 1056.70, entry 984, +7.4% unrealized. SL already at 1035 (above entry, locked +5.2% gain). 1% gap to CMP: 1056.70 × 0.99 = 1046.13. Current 1035 is below that floor (~2% gap), keep as is — trailing tighter would force exit. HOLD with current SL."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",

    {
      "action": "MODIFY",

    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",

    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "HAL",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.70,
      "timeframe": "SWING",

    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",

    },

    {

      "product": "CNC",

  "position_actions": [

    },

  "watchlist_notes": "HINDPETRO — BEARISH research overhang (UBS downgrade, marketing margin losses, fire incident, Q4 today is binary): NO entry. BIOCON — Q4 results TODAY + FDA Form 483 + CEO resignation: avoid new entry. AUROPHARMA / NEULANDLAB / SRF — strong action but pharma/chem already covered via NAVINFLUOR; no need to stack chemicals cluster. BAJAJ-AUTO — buyback record date proximity, prior failure mode flagged; pass. POLYCAB / GABRIEL / CHOLAHLDNG — interesting setups but book is already deployed at ~80% via 11 names; no room without rotation. M&M / DABUR — let post-earnings dust settle.",
  "portfolio_notes": "Book is heavily deployed across 11 CNC names. All positions show favorable price action today, with SLs at or near the 1% gap floor — this is the appropriate posture: locked gains, no forced exits via over-tight trailing. Cluster check: Capital Goods/Defense (HAL + GRSE + TITAGARH = ~3 names, but distinct sub-sectors — aerospace, shipbuilding, rail/shipbuilding) — borderline; will not add another defense/CapGoods name. Financial Services cluster (PNBHOUSING + ABCAPITAL + CAMS = 3 names) at the limit; no more financials today. New entry capacity is limited and most strong setups are in clusters already represented. NO new BUYs this tick — trail discipline and existing-book audit is the right call. Cumulative experiment P&L -53.7% requires capital preservation as priority #1; do not chase extended breakouts."
